info.title: Wardnet DDNS API
info.description: Wardnet DDNS service — regional DNS reconciler (provisioner + reaper) + report-IP/ACME

put.description: Set the DNS-01 ACME challenge TXT records for the calling daemon's network. Creates one `_acme-challenge.<slug>.<parent>` TXT record per supplied value. Returns 409 if the network is not yet active.

409.description: Network not yet active, or a concurrent ACME update was in flight; retry

delete.description: Remove the DNS-01 ACME challenge TXT records for the calling daemon's network. Idempotent — safe to call even if none is set.

204.description: TXT records deleted (or were already absent)

409.description: A concurrent ACME challenge update was in flight; retry

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns 200 OK when the bridge process is running. No authentication required. Used by load balancers and uptime monitors.

put.description: Report the calling daemon's current public IPv4 address. DDNS updates the network's A record in place (it never creates one — the provisioner does that) and stores the reported IP. The target network is the token's `net` claim.

ErrorBody.description: JSON body returned for every error response.

ip.description:
The daemon's current public IPv4 address, e.g. `"93.184.216.34"`.
Must be a globally routable unicast address (private/reserved/multicast
addresses are rejected with `400`).

values.description:
The ACME DNS-01 challenge token values (raw, no quoting needed). A
**per-user wildcard certificate** authorizes its apex and wildcard SANs
through the same `_acme-challenge` name, so this carries one value per SAN
(typically two), published as that many TXT records at once.
